# Generated Markdown Views

Generated Markdown views let agents and humans browse the atlas without using the CLI or MCP server.

## Default output directory

```text
docs/agents/
  atlas.md
  domains/*.md
  workflows/*.md
  components/*.md
  resources/*.md
  verification.md
```

Repos may also opt into a generated root `README.md` through
`agent-atlas.maintenance.yaml`:

```yaml
generated_readme:
  path: README.md
  auto_regenerate: true
```

## Header convention

Generated files, including generated READMEs, should begin with:

```md
<!-- Generated by Agent Atlas. Do not edit directly. -->
```

## Root view

`docs/agents/atlas.md` should include:

- project/repo purpose if available
- domain list
- major workflows
- major systems/components
- how to resolve paths
- how to generate a context pack
- public/private profile notice
- redacted private URI values in public profile output

## Entity card view

Each generated card should include:

- title and ID
- summary
- tags/status/visibility
- key relations grouped by type
- code paths if relevant
- external resource references if relevant
- recommended verification scopes
- agent loading hints

## Keep generated views compact

Generated Markdown is for routing, not total documentation. Link to source artifacts instead of copying them.

## README renderer

The generated README is a compact entrypoint over the same graph. It should use
visible `repository:*` metadata for the title and purpose, route agents to Atlas
commands before broad search, list the most relevant workflows, implementation
surfaces, docs, and verification scopes, and state that README-worthy durable
facts belong in Atlas metadata or referenced canonical docs instead of manual
README prose.
